title: UndoBootstrapResult
description: Output of bin/undo-bootstrap.sh. Reports what was reversed (or what would be, in preview mode) when undoing a bootstrap/retrofit run captured in a BootRecord. Distinct from undo-result.schema.json (commit-undo).

oneOf.title: UndoBootstrapApplied
oneOf.description: Emitted on a real run (status='undone') or a dry-run / preview-without-yes (status='preview').

status.description: 'undone' after a real reversal; 'preview' in --dry-run or when --yes was withheld.

target.description: Absolute path of the repo the boot record describes.

manifest_path.description: Absolute path to the BootRecord manifest.json the run consumed.

source.description: Mirrors the source field on the BootRecord — tells the operator whether they reversed an initial bootstrap or a retrofit-driven remediation.

scope_applied.description: Categories actually reversed. When the operator passed --scope=all (default) this is the canonical 6-element list. Narrower scopes contain only the categories that were processed.

restored.description: Files whose pre-state bytes were restored from pre-state/.

deleted.description: Files removed because the bootstrap run created them (pre_existed=false in the manifest).

branches_dropped.description: Long-lived branches removed (e.g., develop under gitflow). Empty when no branches were created or all created branches have moved past base_sha.

seed_commits_undone.description: Seed commit SHAs that HEAD was reset away from. Empty when bootstrap didn't make a seed commit, or when HEAD has commits stacked on top.

skipped.description: Actions that could not be reversed (file modified post-bootstrap, branch has new commits, etc.). --force overrides most of these.

oneOf.title: UndoBootstrapRefused
oneOf.description: Emitted when undo refuses outright (no manifest found, plan_sha mismatch, target on long-lived branch with stacked work, etc.).
